profile.name: Rampelli Sai Eshwar
profile.role: AI Systems Engineer (LLMs, Computer Vision, Distributed Systems)
profile.tagline: Designs autonomous AI systems using multi-agent orchestration, real-time pipelines, and scalable backend architectures.

projects.title: Barney
projects.problem_statement: Single-LLM systems struggle with complex multi-step tasks due to lack of planning, memory, and tool integration.
projects.solution: Built a modular multi-agent system that decomposes tasks, assigns them to specialized agents, and executes them using tools and memory-aware reasoning.

processing_pipeline: 3. Select appropriate agents for each subtask
6. Run tasks in parallel or sequential flow

decision_logic: Agent selection based on task type (reasoning vs retrieval vs execution)
Dynamic tool selection using LLM reasoning
Fallback handling when tool fails or returns incomplete data

architecture_components.orchestrator: Controls task flow, decomposition, and coordination between agents
architecture_components.memory: Vector database for semantic retrieval and persistent context
architecture_components.execution_engine: Async pipeline supporting parallel and sequential tasks

data_flow: User Input → Orchestrator → Agents → Tools/Memory → Aggregation → Response
constraints_and_tradeoffs: Latency vs accuracy in multi-step reasoning
Token cost due to multi-agent communication
Consistency of memory retrieval vs speed
failure_handling: Fallback to LLM-only response when tools fail
Partial result aggregation if some agents fail

projects.title: Gravity Edits
projects.problem_statement: Manual video editing is time-consuming and requires technical expertise.
projects.solution: Developed a two-stage AI pipeline (Inspector-Director) to convert natural language prompts into executable video editing workflows.

processing_pipeline: 2. Director converts user intent into structured editing plan

decision_logic: Mapping user intent to editing operations

projects.title: VirBoard
projects.problem_statement: Traditional input devices limit natural interaction for writing and drawing.
projects.solution: Built a gesture-controlled virtual writing system using real-time hand tracking.
decision_logic: Gesture classification based on finger positions

projects.title: VidLingo
projects.problem_statement: Manual subtitle creation is slow and does not scale across languages.
projects.solution: Built an automated pipeline for subtitle generation, translation, and synchronization.

projects.title: TelePort
projects.problem_statement: Fragmented OTT platforms make content discovery inefficient.
projects.solution: Created a unified Android TV launcher aggregating multiple OTT services with deep link optimization.
